description: Egg-shaped achenes about 0.1 of an inch long, lustrous, and black or brown. The achene is enclosed within a leathery top-shaped calyx base which may or may not be winged.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: subtropical
care.tips: Similar plants: Halesia carolina | Native to: ORE, WAS, WESTERN CANADA, WAS OO, SOUTHWESTERN USA
Native to: NORTHWESTERN USA, UZB, NORTHERN AMERICA, MXN BC, MXN

description: Elongated achene which is less than 1/2 inch long and has a dry hard covering.

care.soil_types: Peat or soil with specific nutrients
care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions, apply 1-2 times monthly in spring and autumn
care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: wet tropical
care.tips: Remove dead and yellowish leaves and withered flowers | Similar plants: Bougainvillea spectabilis | Native to: NCB, BZC GO, BZE PB, ALG, BZE FN
Native to: BZC GO, BZE PB, IND GO, EASTERN ASIA, BZE FN

description: In mathematics, a pullback is either of two related processes: precomposition and fiber-product; precomposition is a special case of the general fiber-product. Its dual is a pushforward.
